description: An escaping closure capturing self strongly so a view controller and its view hierarchy never deallocate.
body: Failure mode: a stored escaping closure, timer, delegate, or Combine subscription captures self strongly while self also retains it, forming a cycle ARC can never free, so the whole view controller leaks each time the screen is shown. Detection signals: memory climbs as the user navigates in and out of a screen; Instruments shows view controllers not deallocating; object counts grow unbounded. Do not strongly capture self in self-owned escaping closures. Safer fallback: capture [weak self] and guard (or unowned when lifetime is guaranteed), use weak delegates, cancel Combine subscriptions, and confirm deallocation in Instruments.
